🚨SHOCKING ESCALATION IN UTAH 

The LDS Church – which donated $25K to build Utah's largest mega-mosque (Utah Islamic Center) and other mosques in the state – is NOW exposed for partnering with Hamas-linked ministries & financing Hamas contractors!

New March 23, 2026, reveals LDS Charities (Church's humanitarian arm) has funneled support to terror-aligned Islamic groups – far beyond what was known.

Key facts:

🔺LDS Charities partnered with Medglobal, an Illinois-based charity that openly boasted of collaborating directly with Hamas’s Ministry of Health in Gaza (2020 announcement lists LDS Charities alongside Hamas-connected Rahma Worldwide and Taliban-linked Islamic Oasis). Medglobal received $200,000 from LDS in 2017 and $1.9 million in 2019, with support continuing in recent years.

🔺 Rahma Worldwide (Michigan charity) has signed contracts with senior Hamas officials, including designated terrorist Ghazi Hamad (who vowed to repeat the October 7 attacks “time and again”). 

Internal Hamas documents from 2022 confirm Rahma’s Gaza director is “affiliated with Hamas.” Aid workers in Gaza wore jackets bearing both LDS Charities and Rahma logos — along with the logo of RIHS, a Kuwaiti group designated by the U.S. Treasury for supporting Al-Qaeda. 

LDS has listed Rahma as a major partner for over a decade, with joint projects in Gaza, Syria, Lebanon, and beyond - continuing even after Rahma’s terror ties became public. In March 2026, Rahma released a video of a new joint LDS-Rahma project in Syria.

🔺 LDS Charities funded Bayader Association, a Gaza-based group that coordinates closely with Hamas ministries and whose staff have publicly praised Palestinian Islamic Jihad terrorists. LDS supported Bayader welfare projects in Gaza (2014–2016), including untraceable cash grants - a method experts say often subsidizes terror recruitment and operations.

🔺 The Church has long collaborated with Islamic Relief (Muslim Brotherhood-linked, designated a terror organization by the UAE), serving as its largest donor after the 2004 tsunami and contributing millions in goods and services.

These partnerships persisted years after the groups’ terror connections were reported. When previously challenged, the LDS Church dismissed concerns as “false” without addressing specifics, claiming no aid was diverted -  yet the collaborations continued.

🔺 This is the same Church that promotes “interfaith friendship” while funding mosque construction in conservative Utah, where conversions are surging and Republican leaders (Gov. title: JAY THANK U AGAIN 🥹🤍   💬: please tell me how to love your own insecurities +
body:
JAY THANK U AGAIN 🥹🤍 

💬: please tell me how to love your own insecurities + recently i’ve been worried about my thoughts and appearance 

🐈‍⬛: i’m not exactly sure what you’re referring to, but it really depends on how you think about it
🐈‍⬛: if you think about why we use the word “flaw,”
🐈‍⬛: it’s because a certain trait of ours causes harm to ourselves or others, so we call it a flaw
🐈‍⬛: things you’re born with can be improved through self management, which is good
🐈‍⬛: but for other issues, it might help to try changing your perspective
🐈‍⬛: everyone in the world has their own shortcomings
🐈‍⬛: but if you think about it well, there are definitely moments when what we call a “flaw” can work as a strength
🐈‍⬛: it’s okay to overcome your flaws! or, on the other hand, you can think about how to use that trait to your advantage
🐈‍⬛: i think our lives, depending on how we see them, can be turned into something with multiple choice answers, or on the contrary, something we solve like an open ended essay.
🐈‍⬛: choosing to change yourself is also important sometimes, but…
🐈‍⬛: i think it’s also important to consider how i can interpret and prove myself right now.
🐈‍⬛: effort always comes with results. fighting!

あっちの店では
やってくれたのに｣

言ったら

本部に
クレームが来たらしい。

｢断るにしても、
もうちょっと…何とか」
オーナーは
私を説得しようとする。

｢でも、隣の駐車場の料金を払うのに、買い物もしないで
『両替して』と言われたら
オーナーも断りますよね？
私のどこが悪いのか
ハッキリさせてやるから
その人と直接対決したいです！｣

と言った店員は私です。

ちなみに
対決は
させてくれませんでした。

title: Isso aqui não é mea culpa. Andreia Sadi está visivelmente desconfortável, incomo
body:
Isso aqui não é mea culpa. Andreia Sadi está visivelmente desconfortável, incomodada, quase como se só faltasse dizer “satisfeitos?”. O erro continua. Não houve correção de verdade, apenas um recuo diante da repercussão extremamente negativa. Foram pressionados porque pegou mal, simples assim. E os dados mostram isso: 84% das publicações criticavam o tal “PowerPoint”.

Pra mim, não mudou nada. Eles nitidamente se sentiram pressionados e fizeram a correção por obrigação. A expressão da Sadi deixa isso evidente. E o fato de não dizerem claramente que Lula não tinha nada a ver só reforça: não queriam corrigir de verdade. Foram pressionados e fizeram apenas para inglês ver.

É por isso que a esquerda precisa estar atenta, monitorando, reagindo e disputando narrativa o tempo todo. Golpe é a Globo!
